# Quickstart: Selective Import (003)

**Branch**: `003-selective-import`  
**Generated by**: speckit.plan

This guide walks through implementing the selective import feature end-to-end in the correct order to avoid merge conflicts and keep each step independently testable.

---

## Prerequisites

- Branch `003-selective-import` checked out
- Backend dependencies installed (`pnpm install` from repo root)
- Local PostgreSQL 16 running

---

## Step 1 — Database Migration

Add `importEverything` and `selectedSubSourceIds` to the `Integration` model.

**File**: `backend/prisma/schema.prisma`

```prisma
model Integration {
  // ... existing fields ...
  importEverything     Boolean  @default(true)
  selectedSubSourceIds String[] @default([])
}
```

Run migration:

```bash
cd backend
npx prisma migrate dev --name add_selective_import_to_integration
```

Verify: `npx prisma studio` → Integration table shows the two new columns.

---

## Step 2 — Extend `IntegrationAdapter` Interface

**File**: `backend/src/integrations/_adapter/types.ts`

Add `SubSource` interface and optional `listSubSources?()` method to `IntegrationAdapter`. Add optional `subSourceId` field to `NormalizedItem`.

```typescript
export interface SubSource {
  id: string;
  label: string;
  type: 'list' | 'calendar' | 'label' | 'folder';
}

export interface NormalizedItem {
  // existing fields...
  subSourceId?: string;  // set by adapter; used for filtering in sync()
}

export interface IntegrationAdapter {
  // existing methods...
  listSubSources?(integrationId: string): Promise<SubSource[]>;
}
```

**Tests**: No runtime behaviour change yet — existing unit tests should still pass.

---

## Step 3 — Implement `listSubSources()` in Each Adapter

Implement in order of API simplicity:

### 3a. Gmail (`backend/src/integrations/gmail/index.ts`)

Calls `GET https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/labels`.

```typescript
async listSubSources(integrationId: string): Promise<SubSource[]> {
  const integration = await prisma.integration.findUnique({ where: { id: integrationId } });
  if (!integration) return [];
  const accessToken = decrypt(integration.encryptedAccessToken);
  const res = await fetch('https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/labels', {
    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}` },
  });
  if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`Gmail labels fetch failed: ${res.status}`);
  const data = await res.json() as { labels: Array<{ id: string; name: string }> };
  return (data.labels ?? []).map(l => ({ id: l.id, label: l.name, type: 'label' as const }));
}
```

### 3b. Microsoft Tasks (`backend/src/integrations/microsoft-tasks/index.ts`)

Calls `GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/todo/lists`.

```typescript
async listSubSources(integrationId: string): Promise<SubSource[]> {
  // fetch integration, decrypt token, call Graph API
  // return lists.map(l => ({ id: l.id, label: l.displayName, type: 'list' }))
}
```

### 3c. Apple Reminders (`backend/src/integrations/apple-reminders/index.ts`)

CalDAV `PROPFIND` on reminders principal URL — enumerate VTODO collections.

### 3d. Apple Calendar (`backend/src/integrations/apple-calendar/index.ts`)

CalDAV `PROPFIND` on calendar home set — enumerate VEVENT collections.

---

## Step 4 — Add Filter Logic to Each Adapter's `sync()`

After existing fetch-and-normalize code, add filter at return point:

```typescript
// At end of sync(), before return:
if (!integration.importEverything) {
  const allowed = new Set(integration.selectedSubSourceIds);
  return items.filter(item => !item.subSourceId || allowed.has(item.subSourceId));
}
return items;
```

Each adapter must also set `subSourceId` on each `NormalizedItem` during construction.

**Test**: Unit test `sync()` with `importEverything = false` and a known sub-source ID list — verify only matching items returned.

---

## Step 5 — Update Integration Service

**File**: `backend/src/integrations/integration.service.ts`

1. Add `importEverything` and `selectedSubSourceIds` to `IntegrationStatusItem` type.
2. Update `listIntegrations()` select clause to include the two new fields.
3. Add `updateImportFilter()` function:

```typescript
export async function updateImportFilter(
  userId: string,
  serviceId: ServiceId,
  importEverything: boolean,
  selectedSubSourceIds: string[]
): Promise<IntegrationStatusItem> {
  const integration = await prisma.integration.update({
    where: { userId_serviceId: { userId, serviceId } },
    data: { importEverything, selectedSubSourceIds },
  });
  // return mapped IntegrationStatusItem
}
```

4. Add `listSubSources()` passthrough:

```typescript
export async function getSubSources(
  userId: string,
  serviceId: ServiceId
): Promise<SubSource[]> {
  const integration = await prisma.integration.findUnique({
    where: { userId_serviceId: { userId, serviceId } },
  });
  if (!integration) throw new Error('Integration not found');
  const adapter = getAdapter(serviceId);
  if (!adapter.listSubSources) return [];
  return adapter.listSubSources(integration.id);
}
```

---

## Step 6 — Add API Routes

**File**: `backend/src/api/integrations.routes.ts`

Add two routes inside `registerIntegrationRoutes()`:

```typescript
// GET /api/integrations/:serviceId/sub-sources
app.get('/api/integrations/:serviceId/sub-sources', async (request, reply) => {
  const userId = requireAuth(request, reply);
  if (!userId) return;
  const { serviceId } = request.params as { serviceId: string };
  if (!isValidServiceId(serviceId)) return reply.status(400).send(/* ... */);
  try {
    const subSources = await getSubSources(userId, serviceId);
    return reply.send({ subSources });
  } catch (err) {
    if ((err as Error).message.includes('not found')) return reply.status(404).send(/* ... */);
    return reply.status(502).send({ error: 'SubSourceFetchError', message: (err as Error).message });
  }
});

// PUT /api/integrations/:serviceId/import-filter
app.put('/api/integrations/:serviceId/import-filter', async (request, reply) => {
  const userId = requireAuth(request, reply);
  if (!userId) return;
  const { serviceId } = request.params as { serviceId: string };
  if (!isValidServiceId(serviceId)) return reply.status(400).send(/* ... */);
  const parsed = importFilterBodySchema.safeParse(request.body);
  if (!parsed.success) return reply.status(400).send({ error: 'ValidationError', message: parsed.error.message });
  const result = await updateImportFilter(userId, serviceId, parsed.data.importEverything, parsed.data.selectedSubSourceIds);
  return reply.send(result);
});
```

Also update `GET /api/integrations` handler to include new fields in the response (they come through `listIntegrations()` automatically after Step 5).

Add Zod schemas to `backend/src/api/schemas/integrations.schemas.ts` (see `contracts/api.md`).

## Step 7 — Frontend Service

**File**: `frontend/src/services/integrations.service.ts`

- Add `SubSource` type
- Add `importEverything: boolean` and `selectedSubSourceIds: string[]` to `IntegrationStatus`
- Add `listSubSources(serviceId)` function
- Add `updateImportFilter(serviceId, filter)` function

See `contracts/api.md` for full type signatures.

---

## Step 8 — `SubSourceSelector` Component

**File**: `frontend/src/components/integrations/SubSourceSelector.tsx` (new)

```tsx
interface SubSourceSelectorProps {
  serviceId: ServiceId;
  integrationId?: string;    // for API call
  importEverything: boolean;
  selectedSubSourceIds: string[];
  onSave: (filter: { importEverything: boolean; selectedSubSourceIds: string[] }) => Promise<void>;
}
```

Behaviour:
1. On mount / expand: call `listSubSources(serviceId)` → populate checklist
2. "Import everything" toggle at top — when enabled, checks all and disables individual checkboxes
3. Search input (visible when `subSources.length > 10`)
4. Save button — disabled until there is ≥1 selection or `importEverything = true`
5. Show `[unavailable]` badge for IDs in `selectedSubSourceIds` not present in fetched list

---

## Step 9 — Update `IntegrationCard`

**File**: `frontend/src/components/integrations/IntegrationCard.tsx`

- Add `importEverything` and `selectedSubSourceIds` props (with defaults)
- Add "Edit import filter" button when `status === 'connected'`
- On click: expand inline panel that renders `<SubSourceSelector />`

---

## Step 10 — Onboarding Integration

**File**: `frontend/src/app/settings/integrations/page.tsx` (and onboarding page)

- After OAuth callback with `?connected={serviceId}`, check for `?step=import-filter`
- If present, open the `SubSourceSelector` panel for that integration automatically
- Pass `importEverything={true}` and `selectedSubSourceIds={[]}` as initial state

---

## Step 11 — Tests

### Backend unit tests (Vitest)

```
backend/src/integrations/gmail/__tests__/gmail.sync.test.ts
  ✓ sync returns all items when importEverything = true
  ✓ sync filters items to selectedSubSourceIds when importEverything = false
  ✓ sync handles empty selectedSubSourceIds gracefully (returns nothing)
  ✓ listSubSources returns mapped SubSource[] from Gmail labels API
  ✓ listSubSources returns [] on provider error (after token refresh attempt)

backend/src/integrations/microsoft-tasks/__tests__/microsoft.sync.test.ts
  (same pattern)

backend/src/api/__tests__/integration-filter.routes.test.ts
  ✓ GET /sub-sources returns 200 with subSources array
  ✓ GET /sub-sources returns 404 when integration not connected
  ✓ GET /sub-sources returns 502 when provider API fails
  ✓ PUT /import-filter saves importEverything + selectedSubSourceIds
  ✓ PUT /import-filter returns 400 when importEverything=false + empty array
  ✓ PUT /import-filter returns 404 when integration not connected
  ✓ GET /api/integrations response includes importEverything + selectedSubSourceIds
```

### Frontend tests

```
frontend/src/components/integrations/__tests__/SubSourceSelector.test.tsx
  ✓ renders sub-sources from API
  ✓ "Import everything" toggle disables individual checkboxes
  ✓ Save is disabled with zero selections + importEverything=false
  ✓ shows [unavailable] for stale IDs
  ✓ search filters visible sub-sources
```

---

## Verification Checklist

- [ ] Migration runs without errors
- [ ] Existing integrations still sync as before (importEverything defaults to true)
- [ ] `GET /api/integrations` includes `importEverything` and `selectedSubSourceIds`
- [ ] `GET /api/integrations/gmail/sub-sources` returns Gmail labels
- [ ] `PUT /api/integrations/gmail/import-filter` saves selection
- [ ] Next sync after filter update respects selection (SC-002)
- [ ] "Import everything" toggle works as default (SC-004)
- [ ] Zero-selection + importEverything=false is rejected (FR-010)
- [ ] Sub-source panel is scrollable and searchable for 10+ items (FR-012)
- [ ] All backend tests pass (`cd backend && pnpm test`)
